summary: SSRF protection testing for webhook system. All 21 backend tests PASSED (100%). Verified: all 5 private/reserved IP ranges blocked (loopback 127.x, class A 10.x, class B 172.16.x, class C 192.168.x, link-local 169.254.x), file:// scheme blocked, valid public URL allowed, SSRF protection also enforced on webhook UPDATE, full CRUD operations (create/read/update/delete), authentication enforcement on all endpoints, and cross-tenant webhook isolation.
critical_code_review_comments: SSRF fix is comprehensive: _validate_webhook_url() correctly checks scheme (http/https only), resolves all IPs via socket.getaddrinfo, and blocks all 14 blocked networks (loopback, private A/B/C, link-local, CGN, documentation ranges, IPv6 reserved).
_dispatch_with_retries() also validates URL before retry loop — prevents SSRF during async delivery even if URL was somehow stored with private IP.
test_webhook() and replay_delivery() endpoints also re-validate URL at delivery time — this guards against DNS rebinding attacks.
Minor: _validate_url() in webhooks.py is a thin delegate to _validate_webhook_url() — clean design.
No issues with tenant isolation for webhooks: tenant_filter is applied on all DB queries ensuring cross-tenant access returns 404.
seed_data_creation: Created and immediately cleaned up TEST_ prefixed webhooks during tests (TEST_ValidWebhook_httpbin, TEST_CRUD_Webhook, TEST_SuperAdminWebhook). All cleaned up via fixture.
context_for_next_testing_agent: SSRF protection fully implemented and verified in /app/backend/services/webhook_service.py (_validate_webhook_url) and /app/backend/routes/admin/webhooks.py. All 21 SSRF+CRUD+Auth tests pass. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_ssrf_webhook_protection.py. Tenant admin: mayank@automateaccounts.com / ChangeMe123! (partner_code=AA). Credentials confirmed working.
passed_tests: SSRF: Blocked on webhook UPDATE (PUT with private IP returns 400)
Valid URL: Created webhook appears in GET list
CRUD: GET /api/admin/webhooks returns 200 with webhooks array
CRUD: POST create + GET single webhook data persists
CRUD: PUT update name persists via GET verification
CRUD: DELETE webhook returns 200, GET after delete returns 404
Tenant Isolation: Tenant admin cannot delete super_admin's webhook (404)
